channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump golfs with Finland's president amid icy relationship with EU
item.description: President Trump joined the leader of Finland, a member of the European Union (EU), for a round of golf Saturday, despite trade tensions with the executive body, which recently announced levies on agricultural products and clothing items imported from the United States slated to take effect in mid-Ap
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syria swears in new transitional government 4 months after Assad's removal
item.description: Syria’s new transitional government has been sworn in nearly four months after the Assad family was removed from power and as the new authorities in Damascus work to bring back stability to the war-ton country.
item.dc:creator: Ghaith Alsayed And Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico bans junk food sales in schools in its latest salvo against child obesity
item.description: Mexico says a government-sponsored junk food ban in schools has taken effect as the country tries to tackle one of the world’s worst obesity and diabetes epidemics.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Faye Hall, American detained by Taliban, has been released
item.description: Faye Hall, an American woman who had been detained by the Taliban since February, has been freed, a source with knowledge of the release told Fox News Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s denial of Mexico’s Colorado River request sparks concerns over future water negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration’s unprecedented decision to deny a delivery of water to Mexico is raising alarm among experts, who fear it could jeopardize future cross-border negotiations in an increasingly thirsty region.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, The Hill

item.title: Death toll in Burma, Thailand earthquake rises to more than 1,600 victims
item.description: The death toll from the devastating earthquake that rattled Burma and Thailand on Friday has risen sharply to more than 1,650 victims. Nearly 140 people still missing, Burma's ruling military said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of protesters again congregated in Istanbul Saturday to how their support for the city’s imprisoned mayor and demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: The pope's doctor says Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry And Trisha Thomas, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
item.description: The Danish foreign minister has scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Philip Crowther, Kirsten Grieshaber And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's Signal scandal fuels growing distrust from allies
item.description: The Trump administration’s sharing of sensitive military information on Signal has shocked allies, while further eroding trans-Atlantic trust thanks to group chat messages from top U.S. officials expressing contempt toward Europe's “freeloading” on security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: United Kingdom could be only G7 nation not to produce its own steel; Chinese owner blames Trump tariffs
item.description: A Chinese steel group that owns British Steel is blaming U.S. tariffs for the potential closure of two blast furnaces that could impact the steel industry in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, a bridge and a dam. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, the head of the military government said in the televised speech.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Denmark in Trump's crosshairs as Vance makes ambitious appeal to Greenland
item.description: Vice President Vance looked to appeal to Greenland by urging it to side with the US, not Russia or China, after it seeks independence from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Trump tariffs on autos, parts imports could add $4,711 to vehicle cost: Economist
item.description: Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns that President Trump's proposed 25% tariff on foreign-made vehicle imports could lead to a dramatic increase in prices and threaten the U.S. auto industry.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Fears of civil war in South Sudan stem from political rivalry between the president and his deputy
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Tensions are rising in the oil-rich east African nation of South Sudan after Riek Machar, who serves as one of the country’s vice presidents, was arrested in the capital.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 1,000
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 1,000 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secret documents on Nazis who fled to Argentina after WWII being declassified
item.description: Argentina will soon declassify all government-held files relating to Nazi fugitives who fled and settled in Argentina after the Holocaust, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe says no to Trump push to lift sanctions on Russia, upending Black Sea ceasefire
item.description: Europe says no to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure some sanction relief for Russia as he tries to make a Black Sea ceasefire deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation
